sql_array: CREATE TABLE numbers(id INTEGER, value1 FLOAT, value2 FLOAT);
INSERT INTO numbers VALUES (1, 2.5, 3.5), (2, NULL, 7.2), (3, 1.1, NULL), (4, 3.0, 3.0);
SELECT id, greatest(value1 + value2, 4.0) AS max_value FROM numbers WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, value1, value2, greatest(greatest(value1, value2) + 5.0, 10.0) AS final_value FROM numbers HAVING final_value > 10.0;
SELECT id, greatest((SELECT SUM(value1) FROM numbers WHERE value1 IS NOT NULL) + (SELECT MAX(value2) FROM numbers), 10.0) AS overall_max FROM numbers;
CREATE TABLE more_numbers(id INTEGER, num1 VARCHAR, num2 VARCHAR);
SELECT mn.id, greatest(COALESCE(NULLIF(num1, ''), '0')::FLOAT + COALESCE(NULLIF(num2, ''), '0')::FLOAT, 25.0) AS combined_value FROM more_numbers mn WHERE num1 IS NOT NULL OR num2 IS NOT NULL;

SELECT n.id, n.value1, n.value2, greatest(n.value1 + COALESCE(mn.combined_value, 0), 10.0) AS enriched_value FROM numbers n JOIN (SELECT id, greatest(COALESCE(NULLIF(num1, ''), '0')::FLOAT + COALESCE(NULLIF(num2, ''), '0')::FLOAT, 25.0) AS combined_value FROM more_numbers) mn ON n.id = mn.id;
CREATE TABLE temp_numbers(id INTEGER, val1 FLOAT, val2 FLOAT);
SELECT id, greatest(val1 + 1.0, val2 + 2.0) AS adjusted_value FROM temp_numbers WHERE val1 IS NOT NULL AND val2 IS NOT NULL ORDER BY adjusted_value DESC;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id, greatest(val1 + 1.0, val2 + 2.0) AS adj_val FROM temp_numbers GROUP BY id HAVING adj_val > 5.0) AS subquery;
